key_drivers: VIX at 16.40 well below panic thresholds (>30)
No imminent macro catalysts over the weekend
S&P 500 only 1.4% below ATH with positive momentum
5_day.interpretation: Extremely low near-term crash risk. VIX is subdued, market structure is orderly, and no acute stress events are on the immediate horizon. Historical base rate for a 20%+ drawdown in 5 trading days is under 0.1%.

key_drivers: Record margin debt at $1.42T creates forced-selling vulnerability
CAPE at 41.38 leaves no valuation cushion
Yield curve flattening to 27 bps signals late-cycle dynamics
20_day.interpretation: Low but elevated versus base rates. Record leverage means any sudden shock could cascade through margin calls. However, VIX term structure and market breadth do not signal imminent dislocation.

key_drivers: Buffett Indicator at record 233.8% — extreme overvaluation
Negative equity risk premium (-2.0%) historically precedes corrections
Private credit defaults at cycle highs (2.3% KBRA, 6.0% Fitch)
Margin debt YoY growth of 53% exceeds dot-com era rates
60_day.interpretation: Moderately elevated. Valuations are at or near all-time extremes while leverage is at record levels. A catalytic event (geopolitical shock, earnings miss, credit event) could trigger cascading unwinding. Base rate for 20%+ drawdown in any 60-day window is ~2%; current conditions warrant a modest premium.

crash_probability.methodology: Based on current VIX level, technical indicators, macro conditions, historical base rates, and market structure analysis.

metadata.data_source: yfinance, FRED, CBOE, CNN, GuruFocus, FINRA, KBRA, Fitch, weather.gov
metadata.disclaimer: This dashboard is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. All data is sourced from public APIs and may be delayed. Past performance and historical patterns do not guarantee future results.

vix.name: CBOE Volatility Index (VIX)
vix.interpretation: VIX at 16.40 is below the historical average and within the normal range, indicating low implied volatility and complacent market conditions. However, low VIX can also precede sharp spikes during unexpected events.

put_call_ratio.name: CBOE Equity Put/Call Ratio
put_call_ratio.interpretation: Equity put/call ratio at 0.56 is below the historical average of 0.65, indicating relatively more call buying than put buying. This suggests bullish positioning, which can be a contrarian warning of complacency.

fear_greed_index.name: CNN Fear & Greed Index
fear_greed_index.interpretation: Fear & Greed Index at 41 is in the 'Fear' zone, suggesting investors are cautious despite markets near all-time highs. This divergence between price and sentiment can indicate either healthy skepticism supporting further gains or early recognition of underlying risks.

dalio_bubble_indicator.name: Dalio-Style Bubble Indicator
dalio_bubble_indicator.interpretation: The composite score of 71 indicates bubble-territory conditions driven primarily by extreme valuations (CAPE 41.38, Buffett Indicator 233.8%), a negative equity risk premium, and record leverage. Sentiment gauges (Fear & Greed at 41, RSI at 46) are the main mitigating factors, pulling the composite below the 80 threshold for 'Extreme Bubble.'

prices_vs_traditional_measures.description: How high are prices relative to traditional measures (CAPE, Buffett Indicator)?

unsustainable_conditions.description: Are prices discounting unsustainable conditions (equity risk premium)?

new_buyer_entry.description: How many new buyers have entered the market (retail participation proxy)?

bullish_sentiment.description: How broadly bullish is sentiment (Fear & Greed, RSI)?

leverage_purchases.description: Are purchases being financed by high leverage (margin debt)?

forward_purchases.description: Have buyers made extended forward purchases (capex, forward speculation)?

dalio_bubble_indicator.methodology: Rules-based proxy of Ray Dalio's 6-gauge bubble framework. Each gauge scores 0-100 using CAPE, Buffett Indicator, equity risk premium, Fear & Greed Index, RSI, and margin debt metrics. Composite is equal-weighted average.
dalio_bubble_indicator.source_note: Rules-based proxy — not Bridgewater's proprietary indicator. Inspired by Dalio's publicly described 6-gauge framework.

shiller_cape.name: Shiller CAPE Ratio (Cyclically Adjusted P/E)
shiller_cape.interpretation: CAPE at 41.38 is 138% above the historical mean of 17.39 and approaching the all-time high of 44.19 set during the dot-com peak. At these levels, forward 10-year real returns have historically averaged low single digits.

buffett_indicator.name: Buffett Indicator (Total Market Cap / GDP)
buffett_indicator.interpretation: At 233.8%, the Buffett Indicator is near its all-time high of 237.8% and 42% above its long-term average. This places the market firmly in 'significantly overvalued' territory by Warren Buffett's own framework.

yield_curve_10y2y.name: 10-Year minus 2-Year Treasury Spread
yield_curve_10y2y.interpretation: The yield curve has re-steepened to +27 bps after prolonged inversion (peak inversion -108 bps in July 2023). Historically, the transition from inversion to re-steepening often coincides with the onset of recession, as the curve un-inverts when the Fed begins cutting rates.
yield_curve_10y2y.recession_signal: The 2022-2024 inversion fired a recession signal. Re-steepening to +27 bps is consistent with late-cycle dynamics. The signal's predictive window (6-24 months post-uninversion) remains active.

sp500_technicals.name: S&P 500 Technical Analysis
sp500_technicals.overall_interpretation: The S&P 500 is in a technically healthy posture: trading above both major moving averages with a golden cross in effect. RSI at 46 is neutral, providing room for further upside without overbought conditions. Drawdown from ATH is minimal at -1.4%.

price_vs_50dma.interpretation: Price is 2.53% above the 50-day moving average ($7,315.63), indicating mild positive momentum without excessive extension.

price_vs_200dma.interpretation: Price is 8.66% above the 200-day moving average ($6,902.63), confirming the long-term uptrend remains intact.

golden_cross.interpretation: 50-day MA ($7,315.63) is above 200-day MA ($6,902.63), confirming a golden cross — a bullish long-term trend signal.

rsi_assessment.interpretation: RSI-14 at 45.94 is in neutral territory, neither overbought nor oversold. This provides room for directional movement in either direction.

drawdown_assessment.interpretation: The S&P 500 is only 1.43% below its all-time high of 7,609.78, indicating minimal drawdown and strong price resilience.

sp500_above_50dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 50-Day Moving Average
sp500_above_50dma.interpretation: 55.3% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 50-day moving average, slightly below the historical average. Breadth has narrowed from 65% earlier this month, suggesting the rally is becoming more concentrated in fewer names.

sp500_above_200dma.name: S&P 500 Stocks Above 200-Day Moving Average (S5TH)
sp500_above_200dma.interpretation: 59.2% of S&P 500 stocks are above their 200-day moving average. This is within the normal range but below the 73% reading from earlier this month (StockDoz), reflecting some breadth deterioration beneath the surface.

advance_decline_ratio.name: NYSE Advance/Decline Ratio
advance_decline_ratio.interpretation: The NYSE advance/decline ratio of 1.70 (3,351 advancers vs 1,970 decliners) on June 18 shows broad participation in the most recent session, a healthy sign for market internals.

overall_breadth_assessment.interpretation: Market breadth is mixed: the A/D ratio was healthy on the latest session, but the percentage of stocks above key moving averages has been declining from recent peaks. This narrowing breadth amid index-level strength near ATH warrants monitoring.

finra_margin_debt.name: FINRA Margin Debt
finra_margin_debt.interpretation: FINRA margin debt reached a record $1.42 trillion in May 2026, surging $495 billion (+54%) year-over-year. This exceeds previous cycle peaks (2021: $935B) by over 50% and represents the fastest pace of leverage accumulation since the dot-com era.
risks: Forced liquidation cascades if markets decline sharply — record leverage amplifies downside moves
Margin calls could accelerate selling in a correction, turning orderly declines into crashes
Investor credit balances have fallen to record lows, reducing the cash buffer available to meet margin calls
finra_margin_debt.regulatory_note: FINRA margin requirements remain at Regulation T minimums (50% initial, 25% maintenance). No additional macro-prudential buffers have been imposed despite record leverage levels.

overview.interpretation: Private credit has grown to $1.8 trillion with default rates at cycle highs. The combination of rising defaults, elevated PIK usage, and gated funds creates systemic risk through interconnections with traditional banking.

default_rates.name: Default Rate
default_rates.interpretation: The KBRA DLD Direct Lending Index default rate has returned to its all-time high of 2.3%, matching the peak from late 2023. Fitch reports the broader private credit default rate at a record 6.0%. KBRA projects the rate will reach 3.5% by year-end 2026.

borrower_health.name: Borrower Health
borrower_health.interpretation: An estimated 42% of private credit borrowers are generating negative free cash flow, up from roughly 30% in 2021. Interest coverage ratios have deteriorated as base rates remain elevated at 4.25-4.50%.

pik_usage.name: PIK Interest Usage
pik_usage.interpretation: Payment-in-kind interest usage has risen as borrowers under stress defer cash interest payments. PIK mechanisms mask true financial distress by capitalizing interest into principal.
pik_usage.trend: Increasing — PIK usage has expanded across middle-market loans as borrowers face cash flow pressure from elevated base rates.
mechanisms: Borrowers toggle from cash to PIK interest to preserve liquidity
Lenders accept PIK to avoid triggering technical defaults
PIK accruals inflate asset values on BDC balance sheets without cash generation

liquidity_risk.name: Liquidity Risk
liquidity_risk.interpretation: Several private credit funds have restricted redemptions as investor demand for liquidity exceeds the underlying illiquid loan portfolios' ability to generate cash. Semi-liquid 'evergreen' structures are under particular pressure.
gated_funds: Multiple BDCs have implemented soft gates on quarterly redemptions
European private debt funds report rising redemption queues
Interval fund structures facing NAV discount pressure
risks: Redemption gates could trigger investor panic and accelerate withdrawal requests
Fire-sale risk if funds must liquidate illiquid positions to meet redemptions
NAV markdowns could cascade across interconnected fund structures

bank_interconnectedness.name: Bank Exposure to Private Credit
bank_interconnectedness.interpretation: Major banks have significant direct and indirect exposure to private credit markets through warehouse lending, fund commitments, and NDFI (non-depository financial institution) lending. Total NDFI exposure exceeds $2.0T, creating transmission channels for private credit stress into the regulated banking system.
bank_interconnectedness.growth_since_2010: +180% growth in bank lending to non-bank financial institutions
bank_interconnectedness.bank_commitments_to_pc_funds: $450B in committed credit facilities to private credit funds
bank_interconnectedness.regulatory_response: Federal Reserve and OCC have increased supervisory focus on NDFI exposures but have not imposed binding limits.

jpmorgan.private_credit: $55B in direct fund commitments and co-investments

citigroup.private_credit_warehouse: $35B in warehouse facilities for direct lenders

goldman_sachs.other_collateral_lending: $160B in securities-based and other collateral lending

shadow_defaults.name: Shadow Defaults
shadow_defaults.description: Distressed exchanges and liability management exercises (LMEs) that avoid formal default filings but impose losses on creditors.
shadow_defaults.interpretation: When including LMEs and distressed exchanges, the effective default rate rises from the reported 2.3% to an estimated 3.7%. These shadow defaults obscure the true level of credit stress in private markets.
mechanisms: Amend-and-extend agreements that reduce recovery values for existing lenders
Drop-down transactions that move collateral beyond reach of original creditors
Uptier exchanges that subordinate existing lenders without their consent

sectors.name: Software & Technology
sectors.risk: High — revenue growth deceleration amid AI transition creates cash flow pressure

sectors.name: Healthcare Services
sectors.risk: Elevated — reimbursement rate pressure and labor cost inflation

sectors.name: Consumer & Retail
sectors.risk: Elevated — discretionary spending weakness and margin compression

sectors.name: Automotive & Industrial
sectors.risk: Moderate — EV transition costs and supply chain normalization

systemic_risk_assessment.interpretation: Private credit systemic risk is elevated but contained. Unlike 2008, exposures are more distributed across institutional investors rather than concentrated in bank balance sheets. However, interconnections through warehouse lending, CLO structures, and bank fund commitments create plausible transmission channels.
systemic_risk_assessment.comparison_to_2008: Key differences from 2008: (1) leverage ratios are lower on individual loans, (2) investor base is predominantly institutional (pensions, endowments) rather than retail, (3) no equivalent of CDO-squared re-leveraging. Key similarities: (1) opacity of underlying credit quality, (2) rising use of financial engineering to defer defaults, (3) regulatory arbitrage between bank and non-bank sectors.
crash_transmission_channels: Bank warehouse lending freezes could trigger forced liquidations across multiple funds
BDC NAV markdowns could cascade through leveraged fund-of-fund structures
Correlation between private credit stress and high-yield spreads could amplify public market volatility
mitigating_factors: Institutional investor base has longer lock-up periods than retail investors
Direct lending structures have stronger covenants than broadly syndicated loans
Federal Reserve backstop facilities remain available if systemic stress materializes
regulatory_gaps: No consolidated reporting of private credit exposures across the financial system
Valuation methodology for illiquid assets remains inconsistent across funds
Interconnections between bank warehouse facilities and non-bank lenders are poorly mapped

near_term_stress.description: VIX at 16.40, neutral RSI, and minimal drawdown from ATH suggest no imminent stress. Market structure is orderly with adequate liquidity.

valuation_vulnerability.description: CAPE at 41.38 (94th percentile historically), Buffett Indicator at record 233.8%, and negative equity risk premium (-2.0%) indicate extreme valuation stretch. These levels historically precede poor forward returns.

technical_regime.description: Golden cross active, price above both major MAs, RSI neutral at 46. Technical posture is constructive with no bearish pattern formation.

market_structure.description: Record margin debt ($1.42T, +54% YoY) and narrowing breadth create vulnerability to cascading forced liquidation. The gap between price performance and breadth is a warning sign.

macro_environment.description: Yield curve at +27 bps is in the post-inversion zone historically associated with recession onset (6-24 month window). Private credit defaults at cycle highs add latent stress.

key_warnings: Buffett Indicator at 233.8% — near all-time record, significantly above any historical fair-value estimate
FINRA margin debt at record $1.42T with 54% YoY growth — exceeds dot-com era leverage acceleration
Negative equity risk premium (-2.0%) — stocks offer less yield than risk-free Treasuries
Private credit default rates at cycle highs (KBRA 2.3%, Fitch 6.0%) with projected deterioration
Yield curve re-steepening post-inversion — historically precedes recession by 6-24 months
mitigating_factors: VIX at 16.40 signals low near-term stress — no panic positioning visible
S&P 500 golden cross and price above both MAs confirms constructive trend
Fear & Greed at 41 (Fear) — Wall of worry supports further upside in the near term
AI-driven capex cycle provides genuine earnings growth for technology leaders
Federal Reserve retains tools to intervene if financial conditions deteriorate sharply

base_case.outlook: Markets grind higher with elevated volatility as AI earnings growth offsets valuation headwinds. S&P 500 finishes 2026 within 5% of current levels.
triggers: Continued strong tech earnings driven by AI capex

correction_case.outlook: A 10-15% correction triggered by an earnings disappointment, geopolitical event, or credit market stress. Recovery within 3-6 months.
triggers: Major tech earnings miss breaks AI narrative
Private credit event triggers contagion to public markets
Yield curve steepening signals imminent recession

crash_case.outlook: A 20%+ drawdown driven by cascading deleveraging, potentially triggered by a margin debt unwind or systemic credit event. Recovery timeline: 12-18 months.
triggers: Sudden VIX spike above 40 triggers systematic selling and margin calls
Private credit fund failures cascade through bank warehouse facilities
Geopolitical shock (Taiwan, Middle East) combined with leveraged positioning

recommendation_framework.conservative_investors: Reduce equity allocation toward the lower end of target range. Overweight short-duration Treasuries yielding 4.2-4.5%. Maintain elevated cash reserves (15-20%) for rebalancing opportunities.
recommendation_framework.moderate_investors: Maintain strategic allocation but consider implementing collar strategies on concentrated positions. Diversify across geographies and factor exposures. Review margin usage and reduce leverage.
recommendation_framework.aggressive_investors: Current momentum and AI tailwinds support equity exposure, but position sizing should account for elevated crash risk. Use VIX below 15 as an opportunity to buy portfolio protection cheaply.

overall_assessment.historical_context: Current conditions most closely resemble late 1999 (extreme valuations, leverage, new-paradigm narrative) and late 2021 (record margin debt, broad retail participation, low VIX). In both cases, markets continued higher for months before correcting, suggesting timing a top remains extremely difficult even when indicators flash warning.
overall_assessment.disclaimer: This assessment is generated from publicly available data for informational purposes only. It does not constitute investment advice. Market conditions can change rapidly and past indicator patterns may not predict future outcomes. Consult a qualified financial advisor before making investment decisions.
